name: Processed cheese (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Processed cheese [Milk and milk products] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 171289 (Cheese product, pasteurized process, American, vitamin D fortified) - macros, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids, cholesterol
USDA FoodData Central FDC 171252 (American cheese) - full directly-measured 18-residue amino-acid panel

Ahmed et al. 2023, Food Science & Nutrition, doi:10.1002/fsn3.2438, PMID 37181312 (HPLC carbohydrates and organic acids in cheese varieties) - lactose, galactose, lactic/acetic/citric/propionic/pyruvic/succinic/orotic acids
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe growing on process cheese are LACTIC ACID (~1.44 g/100g, previously missing) and LACTOSE (~2.3 g/100g from added dairy solids), followed by acetate; free amino acids / casein-derived glutamate+proline provide N and additional C, and milkfat fatty acids (palmitate, oleate, plus free short-chain C4-C10) are a lipid substrate. The matrix is extremely sodium-rich (emulsifying salts) and citrate can be elevated where sodium-citrate emulsifier is used. Macr
curation_added: lactic acid (1.44 g/100g - DOMINANT organic acid, was absent)
citric acid (native ~0.06 g; note sodium-citrate emulsifier can raise it)
quantitative individual sugars (lactose, galactose) with amounts
full quantitative amino-acid panel (all 18 residues from the directly measured USDA American-cheese panel)
niacin, thiamine, folate, choline, copper, manganese quantities

curation_removed.name: Nickel
curation_removed.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient of cheese; FooDB predicted.

curation_removed.name: Co2+
curation_removed.reason: free cobalt ion is a FooDB trace/predicted artifact; cobalt is relevant only as the B12 core, which is already captured by cyanocobalamin.

curation_removed.name: Molybdate
curation_removed.reason: negligible/trace in dairy, FooDB predicted; not a defining growth nutrient.

curation_removed.name: Fluoride
curation_removed.reason: trace, non-nutritive; not a growth-relevant nutrient of cheese.

curation_removed.name: D-Serine
curation_removed.reason: wrong stereoisomer - food protein contains L-serine, not D-serine; replaced by L-serine.

curation_removed.name: Iron (Fe3+)
curation_removed.reason: redundant duplicate iron entry; consolidated into a single 'Iron' component (0.48 mg).

curation_removed.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
curation_removed.reason: FooDB compartment-tagged duplicate of iron; consolidated into 'Iron'.

curation_removed.name: Gamolenic acid
curation_removed.reason: gamma-linolenic acid is not a genuine constituent of ruminant milkfat; FooDB predicted/implausible.

curation_removed.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
curation_removed.reason: EPA is at best a trace in dairy fat; the listed presence overstates it - FooDB predicted, drop as noise.

curation_removed.name: Doconexent
curation_removed.reason: DHA is a trace-only ruminant-milk component; FooDB predicted, drop as noise.

curation_removed.name: Starch C12H20O10
curation_removed.reason: standard pasteurized process American cheese contains no added starch; FooDB predicted (relevant only to specific starch-stabilized 'cheese product' variants).
